def: s.f. = S کرتويتو कर्तव्यत्व kartavyatva, s.m. Necessity, obligation, obligatoriness; propriety, fitness; practicability; business; suitable measures.
s.m. = S کرتويتا कर्तव्यता kartavyatā, s.f. Necessity, obligation, obligatoriness; propriety, fitness; practicability; business; suitable measures.
s.f. The skin of a calf stuffed and placed near a cow that has lost her calf (to make her give milk), a factitious calf.
s.m. (f. -trī), Doer, maker, &c. ( = kartā, q.v.)
subs: s.f. An active verb (transitive or intransitive)
s.f. A verbal noun of agency

def: s.f. = S کرترتو कर्तृत्व kartr̤itva, s.m. The act, or the property, of an agent; agency, management, government, rule.
s.m. = S کرترتا कर्तृता kartr̤itā, s.f. The act, or the property, of an agent; agency, management, government, rule.
s.m. The seventh month of the Hindūs, Oct.-Nov. (when the moon is full and near Kr̤ittikā or the Pleiades)
subs: (˚ka+ut˚), s.m. The day of full moon in the month Kārttik; a Hindū festival.

def: s.m. The god of war, and son of Siva (so called because reared by the six Kr̤ittikās or Pleiades).
adj. Of or relating to the month Kārttik.
s.m. A cartridge.
s.f. Deed, action; business; duty, obligation; behaviour, conduct; artifice, art, trick, finesse
subs: To be put on one's mettle ( = apnī bānī-par anā).

def: s.f. Deed, action; business; duty, obligation; behaviour, conduct; artifice, art, trick, finesse
subs: To be put on one's mettle ( = apnī bānī-par anā).

def: s.m. A maker; a doer; an agent; an artificer, artisan, artist; a mechanic ( = kārukar, q.v.).
(dialec.) = kisī-kā, gen. of koʼī.
s.m. An earthen pipkin, a pot with a spout; — a pod or seed-vessel
subs: s.f. A Hindū festival (in the month Kārttik) at which every married woman observes a fast and worships the karuʼā filled with water.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Bitter; acrid; pungent; sharp; saline, brackish; unpleasant, disagreeable; harsh; strong; virulent; coarse; hard (of heart, &c.); impetuous, hot (temper); brave; sour, morose; angry, displeased; — s.m. Myrrh
subs: s.m. Oil made from mustard seed, mustard-oil
To harden the heart; to have a brave heart; to bear or suffer meekly
or kaṛwā-nīm, adj. Bitter as gall
(-ko), To embitter; to harden; — to give or to expend unwillingly
(or kaselā), adj. (f. -ī), Bitter and astringent; hard and cruel, adverse; — kaṛwe-kasaile din, Hard and cruel times, evil days
(-ko), To taste bitter, or acrid, or pungent, &c.; to be disagreeable, or displeasing, or offensive (to)
v.n. To be or become bitter; to be or become sour, or surly, or displeased, or angry
or kaṛwī khićṛī, s.f. The food sent for three days by the relatives of a family in which a death has occurred.

def: s.m. A cherub (Ar. pl. karūbiyūn, contrac. obl. karūbīn; P. pl. karrūbiyān, the cherubim).
s.m. = کارو kāru, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Working, doing work; — a worker; an agent; a maker; an artificer; a mechanic; an artisan.
s.m. A person supposed to be the same as Korah (whom the Muhammadans describe as the cousin of Moses; on account of his riches and avarice, his name is proverbially applied to all misers).
s.f. Compassion, pity, mercy, tenderness, benignity, clemency, kindliness
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Compassionate, merciful, tender; — a compassionate person, one who is intent upon compassionateness
(˚ṇā+āt˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Compassionate, tender-hearted; — a compassionate person, &c.
(˚ṇā+āk˚), s.m. 'A mine of compassion or tenderness,' a very compassionate person
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Abounding in compassion or pity, compassionate, tender-hearted, gentle; — a compassionate person, &c.
or karuṇā-nidhi, or karuṇāyan, vulg. karuṇā-āyan, s.m. 'The receptacle or abode of mercy or compassion,' an epithet of the Deity.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁂𑀇 (karei), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁄𑀢𑀺 (karoti), from Sanskrit क॒रोति॑ (karóti), from कृ॒णोति॑ (kṛṇóti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kr̥náwti, from Proto-Indo-European *kʷer- + *-néwti. Cognate with Punjabi ਕਰਨਾ (karnā), Nepali गर्नु (garnu), Persian کردن (kardan).

def: adj. (f. -ikā), Compassionate, tender, kind.

ety: for A. قارورة qārūrat, v.n. fr. قرّ 'to rest, settle,' &c.
def: s.m. A bladder-shaped glass bottle, in which the urine of a sick person is sent to a (Muhammadan) physician for examination, a glass urinal; — (hence) urine (esp. of a patient).
adj. = kaṛuʼā, q.v.

ety: kaṛwā + āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. अवट्टी = S. अ+वृत्तिः
def: Bitter taste; bitterness; pungency; acidity; sharpness; virulence, &c. (see kaṛwā).
s.m. Causer, doer, &c. ( = karne-wālā); — a worker, operative, one who acts as the instrument in bringing about any result.
s.m. An oar, a paddle; a pole used as a rudder; a rudder, helm; — sword.
s.m. A caravan, a large company of travellers, or merchants, or pilgrims, united for mutual protection on a journey
subs: s.m. The chief of a caravan
s.f. A public building for the reception of caravans, a caravansary; an inn.

ety: karwā+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā
def: v.n. To become bitter, &c.; to feel bitterness or anger (towards, -se), to be averse (to), to dislike; (the eyes) to feel sore, or to burn (from want of sleep, &c.).
v.t. To cause to be done, &c., to have or get done; to cause (one) to do or make, &c. ( = karānā).
ex: میری اُس سے مُلاقات کَرواؤ گی کْیا؟
merī us se mulāqāt karvāo gī kyā?
Will you introduce me to her?
wety: Causative form of کرنا (karnā) with stem augmented by وا (vā)

def: s.f.(?) A crane; a heron.
s.m. A bustard; a crane; the stone-curlew, or bastard floriken.
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to a caravan; — one who belongs to or forms part of a caravan.
s.f. The twentieth part of a biswāṅsī, q.v.

ety: kaṛwā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Bitterness, pungency, &c. ( = kaṛwāhaṭ, q.v.).
s.m. An oar, &c. ( = karwāl, q.v.); — karwāra, The bucket attached to a ḍheṅklī (for raising water from a well).
s.m. = karoṛā, q.v.
s.m. An oar, &c. ( = karwāl, q.v.); — karwāra, The bucket attached to a ḍheṅklī (for raising water from a well).
s.m. An oar, &c. ( = karwāl, q.v.); — karwāra, The bucket attached to a ḍheṅklī (for raising water from a well).
s.f. = kaṛwāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. Lying on one side; the position of lying or sleeping on the side; turning from one side to the other
subs: (or karwaṭeṅ) badalnā, or karwaṭ karnā, or karwaṭ lenā, To change sides, to turn from one side on to the other (in sleep), to turn over; to go over to the other side, join the opposite (or the enemy's) side.

def: s.f. = kaṛwāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = karbī, or kaṛbī, or kaṛwī, qq.v.
s.f. = kaṛbī = karbī, q.v.
s.m. Action, operation, work, business, affair; occupation, profession; possession; — cause, origin; motive, object (syn. kāj and kāraj, qq.v)
subs: vulg. kāryadhyaksh (˚ya+adh˚), s.m. A superintendent of work
s.f. The accomplishment of a purpose, the attainment of an object, success
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Active, operative, availing, of avail, effectual, efficient; — a workman, an operative; an agent
s.m. A worker; a manager an agent, a negotiator, an ambassador, a representative.

ety: for A. قرية qaryat, v.n. fr. قري 'to receive hospitably,' &c.
def: s.m. A village; a town.
s.m. The being an effect; the relation or condition of an effect, or of what has been effected.

ety: inf. n. of قرض 'to cut'; 'to repay,' &c.
def: s.m. Debt; a loan, money borrowed at interest; credit; — adv. On loan or credit
subs: or qarẓ adā karnā, v.n. To discharge or pay a debt
(-kā), To contract or incur a debt
To discharge or pay off a debt
s.m. Money lent (or a loan obtained) without interest, and repaid at the pleasure of the borrower
adj. & s.m. Indebted; in debt; — a debtor
s.f. The being in debt, indebtedness; pecuniary embarrassment
(-ko), To grant a loan (to), to lend (at interest); to supply on credit
(-kā), To have a debt (of), to owe
(-ko), To free from debt; to redeem (in law)

(with gen. -kā of amount), To contract or incur a debt (of); to take a loan (of, and from, -se, a person), to borrow (at interest); (with nom. form. of acc.) to take on loan; to take on credit; to run up an account or bill
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قرض (qarz), from Arabic قَرْض (qarḍ).

ety: for A. قرضة qarẓat, n. of un. fr. qarẓ, q.v.
def: s.m. A debt; a loan ( = qarẓ).
adj. & s.m. Borrowed, got as a loan; on credit; — a debtor; a borrower.
A species of grass, of which ropes, &c. are made, Saccharum spontaneum.

s.m. (dialec.) Bell-metal ( = kāṅsā, q.v.); — a small copper coin (called, by Europeans, 'cash'), current, until within late years, in Southern India (eighty kās made one fanam, and twelve fanams a rupee).
s.f. A kind of grass, Saccharum spontaneum ( = kās, q.v.); — s.m. Cough, &c. (corr. of kās, q.v.).
s.m. Assay, test, proof; — the decoction of a colouring substance; extract; essence, virtue; strength, power; tenacity; labour, toil.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُس (kus, “pussy, cunt”).

def: pron. Someone, somebody, anyone, one; a person, a man, an individual
subs: Everybody, noble and plebeian, gentle and simple
adj. Without a friend, helpless, indigent
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُس (kus, “pussy, cunt”).

def: (in H. poetry, and in Braj) = kaisā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُس (kus, “pussy, cunt”).

ety: prob. fr. the Pers. kās, cf. kāsa
def: s.m. A cup, a goblet, a bowl.
s.m. A whip, lash.
s.m. A cough; catarrh
subs: (˚sa-ālu), s.m. An esculent root, a sort of yam
adj. (f. -ā), Removing or alleviating cough, pectoral
s.f. A sort of prickly nightshade, Solanum jacquini
s.m. The plant Cassia sophora (used as a remedy against ccugh)
s.f. 'Cough-destroyer,' name of a thorny plant used as a remedy for cough.

ety: said to be fr. A. کاسة kāsat; but prob. orig. Pers. and = S. कांस्य or कंस
def: s.m. A cup, goblet, bowl; a plate; a trencher; — a royal meal
part. adj. (f. -ī), Tightened, drawn tight; compressed; closely packed; tight, tense, straight; — under weight, barely, scarcely, less than, less
subs: adj. (kasī-kasāʼī), Ready tied, ready packed; strapped; tight, compact.

def: s.m. A reed; a reed-pen; anything hollow (like a reed); a tube, pipe; a bone; a duct, &c.; — a sort of fine linen cloth (made in Egypt)
subs: s.f. The wearing, or being dressed in, fine linen.

ety: prob. for A. قصابة qaṣābat, fr. قصب 'to cut'
def: s.m. A handkerchief tied round the head (worn by women principally).
s.m. The not being in demand, want of currency; badness (of markets), cheapness (of merchandise), unsaleableness, flatness, dulness (of markets); penury.

ety: for A. کثافة, inf. n. of کثف 'to be thick,' &c.
def: s.f. Density, thickness, grossness; abundance; fulness, repletion; foulness, impurity.

ety: for A. کسافة v.n. of کسف 'to be obscured,' &c.; see kusūf
def: s.f. Obscuration; eclipse (of the sun, &c.); — sediment; grossness, impurity, foulness.

ety: kasā(nā)+āwaṭ or āhaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Tightening; tightness; — an astringent.
s.f. Tightening; tightness ( = kasāʼo, kasāwaṭ).
s.m. corr. of قصائي qaṣāʼī, q.v.
corr. of قصائي qaṣāʼī, q.v.

ety: prob. corr. fr. A. qaṣṣāb, q.v.; — ī = S. aff. इक
def: s.m. A butcher; — adj. Hard-hearted, cruel, relentless
subs: s.m. 'A butcher's cat'; (met.) a well-fed bull; a fat person
To look at (one) with the eyes of a butcher; to look fiercely (at)
s.m. The quarter (of a town) in which butchers live.

def: s.m. Odes.
s.m. Odes.
adj. (f. -ī), Astringent; pungent.
s.m. Astringency ( = kasāʼo).
s.f. A butcher's wife; a woman who keeps a butcher's shop; — a cruel or hard-hearted woman, an unfeeling wretch.
s.f. A butcher's wife; a woman who keeps a butcher's shop; — a cruel or hard-hearted woman, an unfeeling wretch.
adj. Of or relating to a butcher (qaṣāʼī); butcher-like
subs: s.m. A sleight in wrestling, by which an adversary is seized by the neck and thrown (as a sheep is thrown down by a butcher).

def: s.f. Lassitude and aching (premonitory of fever, &c.), dragging or breaking pain (in the limbs); pain, ache; a stitch
subs: or kasak nikālnā (-kī), To relieve the pain (of); to assuage pain.

def: v.n. To feel lassitude and aching of the bones; to be racked with pain; to suffer pain.

ety: inf. n. of کسل 'to be sluggish,' &c.
def: s.m. Sluggishness, slowness, laxness, backwardness; indolence, laziness; cowardice; relaxation, lassitude, languor, depression (of spirits); sickness, indisposition; — non emittere in coitu
subs: adj. Relaxed, languid; sick, indisposed, ailing
adj. Sluggish, lazy; cowardly; languid, &c. (see kasal-mand).

ety: for A. کسالة kasālat, inf. n. of کسل 'to be sluggish,' &c.; — kasālat is prob. post-classical
def: s.m. Heaviness, slowness; languor; sickness; grief, trouble, affliction, distress; — hard work, laborious and oppressive business, labour, drudgery, great toil and pains
subs: To labour hard, to toil and sweat, to drudge.

ety: for A. کسالة kasālat, inf. n. of کسل 'to be sluggish,' &c.; — kasālat is prob. post-classical
def: s.m. Heaviness, slowness; languor; sickness; grief, trouble, affliction, distress; — hard work, laborious and oppressive business, labour, drudgery, great toil and pains
subs: To labour hard, to toil and sweat, to drudge.

ety: for A. کسالَي kasālā, pl. of kaslān, fr. kasal, q.v.
def: adj. Lazy, slothful; slow, heavy, languid, indisposed, sick.
s.m. See s.v. kāsa or kās, 'cough.'

ety: v.n. fr. قسم; see qasāmat, and qismat
def: s.f. An oath
subs: (-ko), To administer an oath (to), to make (one) swear
(-ko), To administer an oath (to); to adjure; to conjure (by, -se); to place or put under an oath of prohibition, to place or put under a ban
(-kī), To take an oath, to swear (by); to make a solemn declaration, to asseverate
(-ko), To administer an oath (to), to make (one) swear
(-se), To put on oath, to require one to take an oath, to have (one) sworn
(for qasam-ā-qasamī), s.f. Swearing both parties; mutual asseveration, swearing on both sides.
ex: What kind of thing is it?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قسم (qism), from Arabic قِسْم (qism, “division, sort”).

ety: for A. قسامة, inf. n. of قسم
def: s.f. The administration of an oath.
adj. & adv. Sworn; on oath; bound by oath
subs: To declare or testify upon oath.

def: adj. & adv. Sworn; on oath; bound by oath
subs: To declare or testify upon oath.

def: s.m. Injury, pain, rack, torment, torture.
s.f. Tightening, tying; tie, fastening; leather thong (of a bucket).

ety: kas˚ = S. कष्, or कृष्; ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cause to assay, or test, or try, or prove; to have (a thing) tested, &c.

ety: kas˚ = S. कंस+ānā, as in kasānā, 'to test,' q.v.
def: v.n. To acquire a metallic taste, to be spoiled (curds, &c.) by standing in a metallic vessel.
v.t. To cause to tighten; — to tie tight, to bind ( = kaswānā).
s.f. A kind of poisonous spider.
s.m. Tightening; tightness ( = kasāwaṭ, kasāʼī); astringency.
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat (syn. laḍḍū).
s.m. = kaserā, q.v.
adj. Tightening; astringent.
s.f. A kind of pickle or chutnee made of oil; — the plant Cassia sophera.
s.f. A touchstone; — assay; test, proof
subs: or kasnā, or lagānā, v.t. To apply to or to rub upon, the touchstone, to test, prove.

def: s.m. (dialec.), A handkerchief tied round the head (on going to bed).

ety: for A. قساوة, inf. n. of قسو 'to be or become hard' (the heart
def: s.f. Hardness of heart; stinginess; — grief, anguish, affliction, anxiety, care, chagrin; melancholy, sadness.

ety: kasā(nā)+āwaṭ or āhaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Tightening; tightness; — an astringent.

ety: inf. n. of کسب 'to acquire, gain, earn,' &c.
def: s.m. Acquirement, acquisition (by labour), earning, gain; industry, employment, occupation, trade, profession, handicraft; art, skill; prostitution, harlotry
subs: To acquire, gain, earn; to follow a trade or profession; — to practise prostitution, lead the life of a harlot.

ety: for A. قصبة qaṣabat, n. of un. fr. qaṣab; rt. قصب 'to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. A large village; a small town (particularly when inhabited by decent people or families of some rank); a township.
(for A. qaṣabāt, pl. of qaṣabat), s.m. pl. Towns; large villages.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a large village, or a town; rustic; boorish; — a villager; a townsman.
s.m. Designation of the class which works in bell-metal (kāṅsā); an individual of that class, a worker in bell-metal (cf. kaserā).
s.m. A weaver.
s.m. An artisan, artificer; a trader; — s.f. A prostitute, harlot, courtezan
subs: s.m. A house of ill-fame, a brothel.

def: s.f. A prostitute ( = kasbī).
s.f. A prostitute ( = kasbī).

ety: inf. n. of قصد 'to intend; to propose to oneself,' &c.
def: s.m. Intention, design, purpose, resolve, aim, object; project, attempt, endeavour; desire, wish, will, inclination
subs: (-kā), To entertain the design (of); to harbour designs (against)

(-kā), To form the design (of), to propose to oneself; to intend, design, purpose, resolve, &c.; to endeavour, strive; to attempt, make an attempt (at or on); to make (a thing or a person) one's object or aim, to aim (at); to pursue; to make (for), journey (towards); to strike (at), form a design (against), to attack, to venture (on)

def: adv. Intentionally, purposely; expressly; voluntarily; wilfully, deliberately.
pron. Somebody, someone.
oblique of kasā-kasāyā, q.v.s.v. kasā.
s.m. A vendor of metallic vessels; a brasier, pewterer, coppersmith, plumber
subs: or kaser-haṭ (kaser, for kaserā), s.m. Brasiers' quarter.

def: s.f. Wife of a brasier.
s.m. A kind of grass, Scirpus Kysoor; — the root of Cyperus tuberosus (which is eaten as a fruit).
s.m. See kaserū.
s.m. (Wind) breaking, or dashing to pieces (a ship, &c.); blowing with a loud noise (wind), thundering.
s.m. A crowd, a multitude.
s.m. A plasterer; a potter.
s.f. The eye-brow; — a slice, cut (syn. phāṅk), piece, bit; a chop, steak; — the bow or pommel (of a saddle, e.g. zīn-kā qāsh)
subs: v.t. To slice, to cut in pieces.

def: conj. Would to heaven, would that, I wish to heaven
s.m. Touchstone; assay; — the decoction of a colouring substance.

ety: rt. of kashīdan; Zend karesh = S. कर्ष
def: part. & s.m. Drawing, pulling; carrying, bearing, enduring; drawer; bearer, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. měḥnat-kash, 'enduring (or one who endures) affliction'); — a pull, a whiff (of a ḥuqqa, &c.)
subs: or kash-ā-kashī, or kash-ma-kash, s.f. Repeated pulling; pulling backwards and forwards, or to and fro; jostling, hustling; bringing and taking away; command after command; commanding and countermanding; great unpleasantness, or grief, or pain; distraction, dilemma, perplexity, difficulty; struggle, contention, wrangle, squabble; attraction, allurement (cf. aiṅć-ā-tānī).

def: s.m. A tortoise; the sign Cancer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَشْف (kašf), borrowed from Arabic كَشْف (kašf). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi کشف (kśf /⁠kaśf⁠/).

ety: kash, q.v.+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.f. A line, streak, stripe.
s.f. See s.v. kash.
s.m. A watchman, guard; porter.
s.m. Testing gold by the touchstone; testing, trying; test (of gold, &c.), trial, proof.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa). Doublet of کرشن (kirśn) and کنھیا (kanhaiyā).

ety: imperf. part. of kashīdan; see kash
def: part. Drawing, pulling, attracting; withdrawing; bearing, suffering, enduring.
adj. Large; copious; ample; much; numerous.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa). Doublet of کرشن (kirśn) and کنھیا (kanhaiyā).

def: s.m. A small house; a house, dwelling, edifice.
s.m. (?), Fibres of the wild date, or the palm tree; rope made of these fibres.
adj. Of or belonging to the city of Kāshān; — s.f. A fine kind of velvet.
s.f. = kasauṭī, q.v.
s.f. An astringent ( = kasāwaṭ, q.v.).

s.m. Astringent flavour or taste; astringency; astringent juice; a decoction, infusion; ointment; gum, resin, extract, exudation (from a tree); the colour red; dirt, uncleanness, impurity; dulness, stupidity; stupor.

ety: inf. n. of کشف 'to open, reveal,' &c.
def: s.m. An opening, a declaration, solution, explanation; a manifestation, disclosure, apocalypse, revelation; inspiration.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَشْف (kašf), borrowed from Arabic كَشْف (kašf). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi کشف (kśf /⁠kaśf⁠/).

def: adj. Disclosed, revealed, manifest.
s.f. Drawing; bearing, enduring, &c. (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. Dragging, drawing, pulling; extracting; distilling.

ety: perf. part. of kashīdan; and = Zend karsta; S. कर्षित
def: part. adj. & s.m. Drawn, pulled, stretched, lengthened, prolonged, long, extended; contracted, pulled in; borne, endured, suffered, experienced; disturbed (in mind), vexed, displeased; sullen; supercilious; — having drawn; having endured, &c.; — a kind of needlework, embroidery
subs: adj. Disturbed or distracted in mind; displeased, offended
or kashīda-qāmat, adj. Tall of stature
or kashīda nikālnā (-meṅ?), To trace figures in needlework (on cloth), to embroider.

def: s.f. Tension; displeasure; misunderstanding; superciliousness.
v.t. To draw, &c.; drawing, dragging, pulling; extending, stretching; enduring, suffering (cf. kashīd, and kash).
adj. Fit to be drawn, &c.

ety: act. part. of کشف 'to open, reveal,' &c.
def: s.m. Detector, discoverer; revealer; explainer, expounder.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کاشف (kāšif), from Arabic كاشِف (kāšif).

def: s.f. A drawing; a pull; attraction; allurement; curve or sweep (of a letter in writing); lingering, tardiness, delay; trial, difficulty, pressure (e.g. kashish-ke din); discord, difference, misunderstanding
subs: To experience hardship, suffer distress, to be hard pressed, or in difficulties

def: s.m.(?) Barley; barley-water; a kind of thick pottage made of wheat flour or barley meal with sheep's milk, to which flesh or wheat is added; sour milk dried.
s.m. The sectarial mark made by the Hindūs on the forehead with sandal, &c. (syn. ṭīkā)
subs: To apply the qashqa (to the forehead).

def: conj. = kāsh, q.v.
s.f. = kać-kol, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. kash.
adj. & s.m. Very clear or explicit; — a detector, discoverer, looser, solver, explainer.

ety: v.n. fr. kāshtan; rt. Zend karesh = S. कर्स् (कृष्)
def: s.f. Ploughing, tilling, cultivating; tillage, cultivation (syn. jot; bo-jot); cultivated land, a cultivated tract; a field, a farm; holding, tenure (of land)
subs: s.m. A tiller of the soil, a cultivator, an agriculturist, a farmer
v.t. (caus. of next), To have or get (land) tilled or cultivated; to farm out (land)
(-ko), To bring into cultivation; to reclaim (land)

def: s.m. Evil; difficulty; bad state of things; penury, want, misery, wretchedness; pain, uneasiness, affliction, acuteness of pain, agony, suffering, distress, hardship; vehemence, violence; inconvenience, trouble, labour, effort, pains
subs: or kashṭ pānā, To suffer pain or distress, &c.
(-ko), To afflict, trouble, molest, persecute, hurt
adj. Accomplishable with effort or difficulty
To make an effort, to take pains or trouble.

ety: perf. part. of kāshtan; see kāsht
def: part. Tilled, cultivated; sown.

ety: see kush, and kusht: — tan = S. तं
def: v.t. To kill; killing.
s.f. A quarter or point of the compass, a cardinal point; space, tract; place; mark, goal, limit, boundary; aim; — a lunar station; — a measure of time.
s.m. Wood, timber; a piece of wood
subs: s.m. A small insect or worm found in decayed wood

def: s.m. Aloe-wood or Agallochum.
adj. Wooden; having wood.
s.f. Test, trial; pain, trouble.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Pained, afflicted, distressed, suffering, in want, &c. (see kashṭ); — a sufferer, one who suffers pain and tedious labour (esp. a woman); a distressed person, &c.
s.f. A ship, vessel, bark, boat, ark, canoe, skiff; a tray; a beggar's plate or pot (so called from its boat-like shape); a wallet
subs: s.m. Master of a vessel; a boatman, bargeman, seaman, sailor, navigator
To ply or to row a boat.

def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Troubled; tried, tested.
s.f. = کاشي kāśī, q.v.
s.f. The modern city of Benares (the most sacred of all the cities of the Hindūs, and their most important place of pilgrimage)
subs: s.f. The capital of the Kaśīs, i.e. Benares.

ety: act. part. of کسب 'to gain, earn,' &c.
def: s.m. A gainer, acquirer, earner; one who toils for his bread; an artisan, artist; a tradesman.

ety: act. part. of قصد 'to intend; to aim at,' &c.
def: s.m. A messenger, courier, an express, a postman, letter-carrier.

ety: act. part. of کسد 'to be slow of sale,' &c.
def: adj. Dull of sale, unsaleable (goods); dull, flat (a market); not selling or passing current; worthless; deficient (in quantity or quality); despicable, of no esteem.

ety: for A. قصيدة qaṣīdat, v.n. fr. قصد; see qaṣd
def: s.m. A poem, an ode (similar in form to, but longer than, the g̠azal).
adj. Of or relating to a courier, &c.; — s.f. Travelling or going as courier, the office or situation of courier.
adj. Thick, gross, dense; opaque; unclean, impure, foul, dirty
subs: s.m. pl. Bad times; dirty weather
adj. & s.m. Of a dirty nature or habits; — a dirty fellow.

def: adj. Thick, gross, impure; — bad (in condition or circumstances), wretched (cf. kas̤īf)
subs: adj. Bad or miserable in circumstances.

def: adj. Broken, rent, split; broken down, weak, infirm.
adj. Sluggish, slothful, indolent, inert; relaxed, languid, feeble; cowardly (syn. ḍhīlā).
adj. (f. -ī), Well strung, well knit together, compact, strong, vigorous.
part. adj. & s.m. Dividing, apportioning, allotting; — a distributor, one who allots
s.m. A pillow, a cushion; — clothing; food, boiled rice.

ety: act. part. of کسر 'to break,' &c.; see kasr
def: s.m. Breaker
adj. Many, numerous; much, abundant, plentiful, copious; superfluous; fruitful
subs: or kas̤īruʼl-ʻiyāl, adj. Having a numerous family

ety: act. part. of قصر 'to be short' (of
def: part. adj. Falling short (of), failing, wanting, lacking; deficient, defective; unable, unequal (to), impotent
subs: adj. Failing in relation or description, unable to describe; defective in respect of meaning; inexplicable
adj. Deficient in tongue or language
(-se), To fall short (of); to fail, to be or become deficient, to cease; to be wanting, to lack.

def: s.m. Green vitriol; green sulphate of iron; copperas.
s.m. Blue, or green vitriol; copperas; sulphate of iron.
v.n. To be spoiled (curds, &c.) by standing in a vessel of bell-metal ( = kasānā, q.v.).
s.f. Grittiness.
adj. (f. -ī), Gritty, sandy (bread or food).
v.n. To be or become gritty; — (local, or dialec.) to feel pain (in the limbs), &c. ( = kasaknā).
adv. Tignt, tightly.
s.m. Mixed metal, bell-metal; (fig.) mixed race or breed (syn. doglā).
s.m. Mixed metal, bell-metal; (fig.) mixed race or breed (syn. doglā).
s.m. = kaskuṭ, q.v.
s.m. = kaskuṭ, q.v.
s.m. = kassā or kussā, q.v.
(inf. n. of قسم 'to divide,' &c.), s.m.(?), Dividing, apportioning, parting, partition.
ex: What kind of thing is it?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قسم (qism), from Arabic قِسْم (qism, “division, sort”).

def: s.f. Twisting, wriggling; fidgeting, restlessness.

ety: prob. fr. S. कृष् and मृष् or मृश्
def: v.n. To move, shake, twist, wriggle, writhe; to fidget, to become restless; to bestir oneself.
s.m. The country of Cashmere (in the north-west of India).
s.f. A woman of Cashmere; a dancingwoman.
adj. & s.m. Born or produced in Cashmere, of Cashmere; — a native of Cashmere; — a dancing-boy; — s.f. ( = S. kāśmīrikā), The language of Cashmere.
s.f. A woman of Cashmere; a dancingwoman.

ety: Prk. कसणअं; S. कर्षणीयं, rt. कृष्
def: v.t. To draw tight, to tighten, to brace; to gird up; to tie, fasten, strap, bind; to press upon, bear hard upon; to raise or increase (in price); to load (a gun); to dry up (the water, or juice, of); to fry (in ghī)
s.m. Brace; braces; bed-string; a pack-cloth; a bundle, a wallet.

ety: Prk. कसणअं; S. कषणीयं, rt. कष्
def: v.t. To assay, try, prove, test, examine.
corr. form of kasnā, q.v.
s.f. Endive, white succory, Cichorium endivia; or chiccory, Cichorium intybus; — adj. Chiccory-coloured; lilac.
adj. & adv. (Braj) = kaisā, q.v.
s.f. A hoe ( = kassī, or dim. of kassā).
s.m. Compelling; compulsion; violence; retaliation, revenge.

ety: inf. n. of قصر 'to be or become short; to diminish,' &c.
def: s.m. Diminution; deficiency; defect, something wanting; — drawing in (of evening); evening twilight; — a citadel; castle; palace, mansion, edifice, building; an elegant villa.

s.f. Breaking; breach; routing, putting to flight; rout; (in Arith.) a fraction; — loss, damage; affliction, trouble, inconvenience; contrition; (in Gram.) the sign (ـِ) of the short vowel i; — (in Hindūstānī, perhaps by mistake for qaṣr, q.v.), something wrong or amiss; something wanting, a want; deficiency, defect, flaw
subs: To make good a loss (to), to indemnify; to make up a deficiency
(-meṅ), Trouble or inconvenience to arise; — a deficiency to occur, to fall short, to fail (in)
(-ko), To inflict loss (on), cause (one) to suffer loss or damage
(-meṅ?), To make a deduction (in), to deduct, to decrease; — to fall short (of), to fail, to be wanting (in)
s.f. The numerator of a fraction
or nikāl-lenā (-kī), To make up a loss or deficiency; to be revenged (for), be quits (with, -se), to pay (one) off (for)
or kasr-ě-ziyād, More or less ( = kam-o-besh).

ety: for A. کسرة kasrat, n. of un. fr. kasr, q.v.
def: s.m. A breaking, a fracture; affliction, &c. (see kasr); (in Gram.) the short vowel (-ِ) i.

ety: for A. کثرة, inf. n. of کثر 'to be numerous,' &c.
def: s.f. Multitude, plenty, abundance, superfluity, excess, glut; plurality, multiplicity, majority, the major part, the best or greatest part; (in Hind.) practice, usage, habit, fashion, vogue, mode (prob. by mistake for kasrat, q.v.)
subs: adv. In abundance, abundantly, plentifully; in great numbers; in excess; — kas̤rat-se honā, v.n. To exist, or be found, in abundance, to abound, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کثرت (kasrat), from Arabic كَثْرَة (kaṯra).

r: kasrat, kasr, kasra, lit., kasrat karnā
def: (for A. کسرة kasrat, 'a breaking'; see kasr, and kasra), s.f. lit. 'Breaking in the body'; training, exercise, bodily or athletic exercise; practice, usage, habit
subs: To train, to practise oneself in athletic or gymnastic exercises.

def: s.f. Breaches; losses, &c. (see kasr).
adj. Usual, current, customary, &c. (prob. by mistake for کسرتي kasratī, q.v.).
adj. Trained; developed by training or by athletic exercises; athletic, gymnastic; practised, familiar, usual
subs: s.m. One accustomed to athletic exercises, an athlete.

def: s.m. The bark of the kīkar or babūl (Acacia); a spirit extracted from the bark of the acacia (cf. kas); — an inferior kind of chick-pea.

ety: intens. n. fr. qāṣib, act. part. of قصب 'to cut, cut up,' &c.
def: s.m. A butcher (see qaṣāʼī).
s.f. The trade of a butcher.
s.f. A hoe; a small mattock, a spade ( = kasolī); — a measure equal to two paces, each pace being thirty-six finger-breadths, measured across the first joints of the first and fourth fingers.
s.m. Name of a plant, Chara (Bruce).
s.m. See kashṭ.

ety: fr. Zend kaśu; Pehl. kaś; cf. kih
def: Decrease, diminution, loss, damage.

ety: fr. Zend kaśu; Pehl. kaś; cf. kih
def: Decrease, diminution, loss, damage.

ety: fr. Zend kaśu; Pehl. kaś; cf. kih
def: Decrease, diminution, loss, damage.
s.m. An oyster, Ostrea edilis.
s.m. (f. -ī), A species of crested blackbird, Merula nigrophileus; — also the Merula bulbul; — a species of thrush, Geocichla cyanotus.
s.f. Musk (the animal perfume, brought from Kashmīr, Naipāl, and Bhūṭān); the musk-bag; the musk-deer ( = kastūrī-mr̤ig).
s.m. Horse-cloth, housings.
s.m. A sickle.
v.t. To cause to be tightened; to have or get tied, or fastened, or packed, &c.; — to cause to be tested, to have (a thing) tested or proved, &c.
s.m. = کشيپ kaśyap, q.v.
s.m. Name of a certain deified sage, son of Marīćī, husband of Diti amd Aditi, and father of the immortals (both gods and demons); — a kind of fish; a kind of deer.
adj. Sufficient, enough (see faqat̤).
s.m. = kāṭh, q.v.
adv. Why? how?
s.m. (in comp.) = kaṭh = kāṭh, qq.v.: — kaṭ-bandhan = kaṭh-bandhan.
adj. (in comp.) = کٿو kaṭu, q.v.

ety: S. क्कथः; or rt. of kāṭnā
def: s.m. A black colour used to mark chintz with.
s.f. The waist; the loins, reins
subs: s.m. = kaṭi-bandhan, q.v.s.v. kaṭi; — kaṭ-keharī, adj. Lion-waisted, having a waist as elegant as the lion's, slim-waisted (woman).

def: s.m. = kaṭ,hal, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of قطع 'to cut; cut off,' &c.
def: s.f. Cutting; a cutting, section; intersection; a segment; a portion, division; a breaking off; intercepting; traversing or passing over (a road, &c.); cut, shape, form, make, fashion, style; stamp; model
subs: s.m. Separation (from, -se), desertion, abandonment; — qat̤ʻ-ě-taʻalluq karnā (-se), To separate oneself (from), to abandon, desert
adj. Of good shape, or cut, &c.; graceful
Getting over a road or journey
To sever the ties of kindred; to break off connexion with relatives
v.t. To cut; cut off; cut out; to form, fashion; to carve; to cut short; to break off; to break with; to traverse, cross, pass or get over (a road, &c.)
v.t. To interrupt the speech of; to put in a word

s.f. Turning away the eyes, averting the regard or attention (from); leaving off attending (to) or considering; abstraction; — adv. Leaving out the consideration (of, -ke); independent (of the fact); irrespective (of), without reference (to); exclusive (of); besides; at any rate; howsoever; albeit; nevertheless
(-se), To be cut, or cut off (by); to be cut short; to be settled, or adjusted; to be deducted (from); to be traversed or passed over (a road, &c.); to be performed or accomplished (a journey, &c.).

def: s.f. A cutting; cleavage; dissection; separation, severance, disconnection; a cut, slash, incision; execution; a bite; a wound; (met.) an untruth, a falsehood; — a section; a chip; a slice, piece, bit, part, fraction, patch, plot (of ground); — a passage, way, channel; — spur (of a cock); — scum, dirt, lees, sediment; — keenness, sharpness; edge (of a sword, &c.); — warmth; pungency; virulence; corrosiveness; damage, loss
subs: s.f. Cutting and clipping; cuttings; deductions, &c. ( = kāṭ-ćhāṅṭ, and kāṭ-kūṭ); backbiting; — kāṭ-phāṅs karnā (-kī), To cut and clip; to backbite

or kāṭ-kūṭ, s.f. Cutting and clipping; cutting and hacking; pruning, lopping; cutting out; curtailing; scratching, scribbling; — cuttings, clippings, chips, scraps; deductions, retrenchments; scrapings, pickings; petty savings; erasures, amendments (in writing, &c.); — kāṭ-ćhāṅṭ karnā, or kāṭ-kūṭ karnā, v.t. To cut and clip; to prune; to cut out; to cut off; to amputate; to hack, mangle, mutilate; to scratch out, make erasures (in), to correct; to make deductions (from), to deduct

s.m. A conditional engagement; a deed of conditional sale (stipulating that if the purchase price be not returned within a given period the sale is absolute); a mortgage deed with liability of sale if not redeemed by a stipulated time
v.n. To make an incision or wound (in), to cut, to wound; to cut a passage (for); to cut away; to corrode; to destroy
s.f. Cutting up, cutting to pieces, a general slaughter or massacre ( = kāṭā, q.v.)
s.f. The diameter (of a circle); the diagonal of a square).

ety: rt. of kaṭnā, or kāṭnā, and contrac. of kaṭā
def: s.f. & adj. (in comp.), Cutting; cut; past, &c. (see kāṭnā, and kāṭ)
subs: s.f. Dahī made of skimmed milk
s.m. Past time, the past; (in Gram.) the past tense; — adj. Of or relating to the past, retrospective

def: adv. Where? whither? what?

ety: S. कर्त, rt. कृत्+कः; cf. kāṭnā
def: s.m. (dialec.), A knife for cutting bamboos with.
s.m. A bite ( = kāṭ).

ety: perf. part. of kāṭnā, and = Prk. कट्टिअओ; S. कर्तित+कः
def: part. adj. & s.m. Cut, cut off; bitten; reaped; spent, passed, &c.; — one who is bitten (by, -kā), &c.

ety: perf. part. of kaṭnā, and = S. कर्तित+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Cut, &c. (see kaṭnā)
s.m. What is spun; thread
subs: s.m. 'Old woman's spinning'; a kind of sweetmeat made of thread-like shreds of candied pumpkin.

ety: S. कर्तकः, i.e. कृत्+अ+कः; — or i.q. kaṭāʼo, q.v.
def: s.m. Cutting up, cutting to pieces; bloodshed, slaughter, general slaughter or massacre
subs: s.f. Slaughter; fight, pitched battle; — deadly enmity
s.f. Cutting and clipping; general retrenchment; — cutting and hacking, general slaughter or massacre
v.t. To cut up, to cut to pieces; to kill, murder, slaughter, massacre.

def: s.m. = kaṭāksh, q.v.
s.m. A frying-pan, a shallow kind of boiler (for oil, butter, sugar, &c.), a caldron ( = kaṛāh, q.v.).
adj. = kaṭahā, and kaṭāsā, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Addicted to biting or snapping, snappish; — a biter (syn. kaṭṭar, kaṭkhanā).
s.f. Spinning; price paid for spinning
s.f. Cutting; reaping; reaping-season, harvest-time ( = kaṭnī), harvest, crop; — price paid for cutting, or reaping
subs: or kaṭāʼī-kī rut, or kaṭāʼī-kī sākh, Reaping-season, harvest-time

def: A thorny plant which bears a purple flower, Solanum jacquini (syn. gul-ḵẖār).
s.m. A prickly shrub growing to the height of ten or twelve feet ( = bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā, q.v.); — a coarse grass growing on fallow-ground
subs: s.m. A plant resembling the common thistle

def: s.m. A bracelet; — an army, a legion; — the side or ridge of a hill or mountain; low land at the foot of a mountain, marshy land, meadow (syn. tarāʼī); — Cuttack (the cap. of Orissa).
s.m. Side-look, side-glance, leer, ogling
subs: v.n. To cast a side-glance (at), to look askance (at); to leer; to ogle.

def: s.m. Flood, spring-tide (Bengal).
s.m. Cutting, clipping; cutting off; excision; amputation, &c. (see kāṭnā).
adv. Cuttingly; conclusively, definitively, &c. ( = qat̤ʻī, q.v.); — in no shape or form, not at all; never.
s.m. A kind of cloth, fine linen (said to be so fine as to be rent by exposure to the moonlight; and hence, is represented by Oriental poets as enamoured of the moon).
v.t. To cause (one) to spin; to get spun ( = katwānā).
v.t. To cause to cut; to cut; to cause to bite; — to cause to be cut, or to be bitten (kaṭwānā).

s.m. Cutting; reaping; harvest, crop ( = kaṭāʼī); a cutting, a section; an excision; a breach; bank (of a river, &c.), river-side; — terseness, or closeness, or nervousness (of language or composition); — sharpness or incisiveness (of speech); cutting speech, jesting, mockery, taunt; — a kind of flowering on cloth, embroidery; — imposing display.

adj. & s.m. Cowardly, timorous, timid, faint-hearted, shrinking, irresolute; gentle; — discouraged; distressed, agitated; confused, perplexed; — a cowardly or faint-hearted person, &c.; — timidity, &c. ( = kātaratva, q.v.); — a large kind of fish, Cyprinus catla.
s.m. A dagger with a broad straight blade, the hilt of which comes up on either side of the wrist, and which is grasped by a cross-bar in the centre; a dagger, poniard, stiletto, dirk
subs: s.m. One who carries a dagger, an assassin
or kaṭār mārnā (-ko), To put a dagger into (one), to stab.

def: s.m. A pole-cat ( = khaṭās, q.v.).
s.f. Cutting, clipping, &c. (used chiefly in comp.); — kattar or katar, s.f. A fillet
subs: s.m. pl. Artifices, wiles, tricks, feints, stratagems, manœuvres
s.m.(f.?) Cutting out (clothes); cutting and clipping; cuttings, deductions; thrift, parsimony; illicit picking, overcharge, peculation; — (met.) meditation; anxiety, disquiet; consultation
adv. By cutting and clipping; by careful management or contriving; by hook or by crook, &c.
(kā or -kī?) To cut out; to cut and clip, to retrench, to deduct (from); to pick and filch, to practise peculation, to purloin; — to plan, devise, contrive.

def: adj. & s.m. Hard, cruel, merciless, relentless; — a hard or cruel man; a caitiff.
s.m. The sound of crunching or munching grain, &c.; crunching, munching
subs: or kuṭur-kuṭur, s.m. Idem; — ady. Crunch-crunch, munch-munch.

ety: prob. S. कान्तारकः; but cf. kaṭārā
def: s.m. A variety of the sugar-cane; — tamarind.
s.m. Name of a medicinal plant, the Globe-thistle, Echinops echinatus; — the aloe plant; — a kind of sugar-cane ( = katārā).
s.m. corr. of قطار qat̤ār, q.v.
s.f. = S کاترتو कातरत्व kātaratva, s.m. Cowardice, timidity, timorousness, fear; agitation; dejection, depression of spirits.
s.m. = S کاترتا कातरता kātaratā, vulg. kātartā, s.f. Cowardice, timidity, timorousness, fear; agitation; dejection, depression of spirits.
s.m. See s.v. katar.
s.f. A small dagger, a dirk, a stiletto; — a dagger-shaped figure (on cloth)
s.m. A kind of silk cloth with stripes in the form of daggers (syn. kaṭārī-dār).

ety: katar˚ = S. कर्तय(ति), rt. कृत् 'to cut'
def: v.t. To cut, clip, pare, lop, prune, trim; to cut out; to cut up, to hew.
s.m. A kind of fish.

ety: fem. of katarnā, q.v., or S. कर्तनी
def: s.f. Scissors; shears.
s.f. Price paid for cutting out.
adj. Side; crooked, awry, zigzag; doubling (as a hare).
v.t. To cause to be cut or clipped, &c., to get or have (a thing) cut, or cut out, or trimmed, or pared, &c.
s.m. = khaṭās, q.v.

ety: kāṭ(nā), q.v.+āsā = Prk. वंछओ = S. वाञ्छकः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Inclined to bite, addicted to biting; snappish; rabid ( = kaṭāhā).
adj. Cutting; biting; sharp, incisive, trenchant; caustic.

ety: kaṭāw(nā) = kaṭā(nā)+atī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आप्तिका
def: s.f. That which is to be de ducted, deduction; discount; exchange.
adj. & s.m. = kaṭahā, q.v.

ety: kaṭauwā, q.v.+al = Prk. अडो = S. लः
def: adj. = kaṭahā, or kaṭṭar, or kaṭkhanā, qq.v.
A thorny plant which bears a purple flower, Solanum jacquini (syn. gul-ḵẖār).
